text.title: Required-everything plus additionalProperties:false
text.summary: In OpenAI/Azure strict mode, every property must be required and every object must forbid additional properties; this is a grammar invariant, not a style choice.

section.title: The rule
section.p: Strict-mode structured outputs compile the JSON Schema into a finite-state grammar. The compiler rejects any schema where the set of required properties is not equal to the set of declared properties, or where `additionalProperties` is missing or `true`. Optional semantics are encoded by making a field nullable, not by removing it from `required`.

rule.statement: For every object schema submitted with `strict: true`, set `additionalProperties: false`, and list every declared property in `required`. To express "this field may be absent", use a nullable type (`{"type": ["string", "null"]}` or Pydantic `str | None`) — never omit the field from `required`.
rule.rationale: The strict-mode compiler builds a regular grammar; optional fields would introduce branch non-determinism (skip token vs read token), and unrestricted additional properties would force the grammar to accept arbitrary tokens between every key. The OpenAI error `400: Invalid schema for response_format ... additionalProperties must be false` is the compiler refusing the schema.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict
class User(BaseModel): model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid") # -> additionalProperties: false name: str email: str middle_name: str | None = None # nullable, NOT optional
example.note: `extra="forbid"` makes Pydantic emit `additionalProperties: false`. `str | None` becomes `{"type": ["string","null"]}` and stays in `required`.
`middle_name` is declared but not required, and `additionalProperties` is missing. OpenAI strict mode returns 400 on submission.

section.reference: Pydantic model wired for strict mode.
Hand-written JSON Schema that passes strict-mode compilation.
OpenAI structured outputs guide — strict-mode constraints.
OpenAI community thread documenting the 400 error.
